hook: District 25 just cracked 5.5% gross yield while Orchard lags at 3.8%

slides.title: Where Rent Beats 5%
slides.subtitle: Q1 2026 URA rental data dissected — four districts, four very different landlord stories.

slides.headline: Wallich Residence 4BR hit $18,000/mo in Jan 2026
slides.footnote: URA rental transaction, Wallich Street, District 02, 1,750 sqft, Jan 2026. Single highest non-landed rent in this dataset.

slides.title: What Q1 2026 Rentals Tell Us
items: D14 Sims Urban Oasis 2BR (650 sqft) median rent: $3,900/mo — tight spread signals strong occupancy demand near Aljunied MRT.
D15 Tanjong Rhu 3BR (1,350 sqft) range: $4,800–$5,600/mo — waterfront premium holding despite more supply entering mid-2026.
D10 Shangri-La Residences: 1,650 sqft units fetching $9,752–$14,856/mo — ultra-luxury rents stable but yield is the lowest of all four districts.
D25 Rosewood Suites 3BR (1,350 sqft) at $4,000–$4,600/mo — lowest absolute rent but entry price gap vs CCR makes the math work for yield hunters.

slides.title: 4 Districts: Landlord ROI Ranked

items.label: D25 Woodlands
items.sub: Rosewood Suites 3BR $4,600/mo on ~$1.0M entry — best gross yield in this dataset

items.label: D02 CBD / Tanjong Pagar
items.sub: Wallich 3BR averaging $11,600/mo on ~$2.8M — high absolute rent salvages the yield

items.label: D14 Geylang / Paya Lebar
items.sub: Sims Urban Oasis 2BR $3,900/mo on ~$975K — volume of leases signals low vacancy risk

items.label: D10 Nassim / Orchard
items.sub: Shangri-La ~$11,000/mo but purchase prices ~$3.5M compress net return sharply

slides.footnote: Gross yield = (median monthly rent × 12) ÷ estimated market value. Source: URA Q1 2026 rental data, Apr 2026.

slides.title: Why D14 Is the Volume King
items: Sims Urban Oasis logged the most URA rental transactions in this dataset — 30+ leases across Q1 2026, indicating near-zero vacancy drag.
1BR units (450 sqft) renting $3,150–$3,700/mo offer the highest rent-per-sqft in D14, ideal for single-asset yield maximisation.
3BR units (850 sqft) jumped to $5,300–$5,500/mo by Q1 2026 — renters absorbing size premium as co-living demand rises post-circuit-breaker.
Entry prices for D14 resale condos remain sub-$1,600 psf — pairing with these rents keeps gross yield above the 4.5% threshold.

slides.headline: D10 Nassim detached house leased at $62,000/mo — but that's landed
slides.footnote: URA data: Nassim Road detached house (>8,000 sqft), District 10, Jan 2026. Landed segment; not comparable to condo yield calc.

slides.title: Chase Yield? Look North First
slides.body: The instinct is to buy in Orchard or River Valley. The data says don't. D25 and D14 are generating gross yields 70–170 bps above prime districts in Q1 2026. Net of ABSD, mortgage and maintenance, every basis point matters. Suburban CCR-adjacent condos — bought right — are outperforming trophy assets on cash-on-cash return right now.
slides.quote: Prestige costs yield. Know which game you're playing before you sign.

caption:
📈 D25 is quietly beating Orchard on rental yield by nearly 2 percentage points.

• Rosewood Suites 3BR hitting ~5.5% gross yield vs D10 Shangri-La at ~3.8%
• Sims Urban Oasis (D14) logged 30+ leases in Q1 2026 — vacancy is almost zero
• Wallich Residence 4BR cleared $18,000/mo — high rent, but price kills the yield

If you're buying for income, suburban beats trophy in 2026. The numbers are unambiguous.
